subject: computer-science
description: Computer Networks MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is a computer network?
options: A collection of computers and devices connected via communications devices and transmission media
mcqs.explanation: A computer network is a collection of computers and devices connected together via communications devices and transmission media.

mcqs.q: A computer system used as a central repository of data and various programmes shared by users in a network is called a:
mcqs.explanation: A network server is a computer system used as the central repository of data and programmes shared by users in a network.

mcqs.q: Two devices are said to be in a network if:
options: A process in one device is able to exchange information with a process in another
mcqs.explanation: Two devices are in a network if a process in one device is able to exchange information with a process in another device.

mcqs.q: A network in which the communication channel is shared by all machines is called:
mcqs.explanation: In a broadcast network the communication channel is shared by all machines on the network.

mcqs.q: Which device forwards packets between networks by processing routing information in each packet?
mcqs.explanation: A router forwards packets between networks by processing the routing information included in each packet.

mcqs.q: A list of protocols used by a system — one protocol per layer — is called a:
mcqs.explanation: A protocol stack is a list of protocols used by a system, with one protocol per layer of the network model.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is considered a network performance issue?
mcqs.explanation: An unauthorized user accessing a network is considered a network performance (and security) issue.

mcqs.q: How long is a MAC address?
mcqs.explanation: A MAC (Media Access Control) address is 6 bytes (48 bits) long, in binary numbers — typically displayed in hexadecimal.

mcqs.q: In what number system is a MAC address fundamentally expressed?
mcqs.explanation: A MAC address is fundamentally a binary number 48 bits long, although it is commonly displayed in hexadecimal for readability.

mcqs.q: Network computer devices that originate, route and terminate the data are called:
mcqs.explanation: Network computer devices that originate, route and terminate data are called network nodes.

mcqs.q: Which device connects multiple nodes to a network?
mcqs.explanation: A hub is a device that connects multiple nodes to the network.

mcqs.q: In a Virtual Circuit Network, each packet contains:
mcqs.explanation: In a Virtual Circuit Network each packet contains the full source and destination address.

mcqs.q: LAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: LAN stands for Local Area Network — confined to a relatively small area such as a room or building.

mcqs.q: A LAN is best described as a network that:
options: Is confined to a relatively small area
Is used by mobile phones only
mcqs.explanation: A LAN (Local Area Network) is a network confined to a relatively small area like an office, room or building.

mcqs.q: Is a LAN card necessary for a standalone computer?
options: No — LAN card is not necessary for a standalone computer
mcqs.explanation: A LAN card is NOT necessary for a standalone computer — it is only required when the computer needs to connect to a local area network.

mcqs.q: MAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: MAN stands for Metropolitan Area Network — covers a city or metropolitan region.

mcqs.q: A Metropolitan Area Network covers:
options: A city or metropolitan-size geographic region
mcqs.explanation: A MAN (Metropolitan Area Network) covers a geographic region the size of a metropolitan area / city.

mcqs.q: WAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: WAN stands for Wide Area Network — covers a state, country or the whole world.

mcqs.q: A WAN typically covers:
options: A state, country or the whole world
mcqs.explanation: A WAN (Wide Area Network) covers a state, province, country or the whole world.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a classic example of a WAN?
mcqs.explanation: The Internet is the largest and most classic example of a WAN (Wide Area Network).

mcqs.q: PAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: PAN stands for Personal Area Network — enables communication between computer devices near a person.

mcqs.q: Bluetooth devices typically form which type of network?
mcqs.explanation: Bluetooth devices typically form a PAN (Personal Area Network) — communication near a single person.

mcqs.q: CAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: CAN stands for Campus Area Network — connects multiple buildings within a campus.

mcqs.q: Which of the following best describes a CAN?
options: Network connecting multiple buildings within a campus
mcqs.explanation: A Campus Area Network (CAN) connects multiple buildings within a campus — common in universities.

mcqs.q: HAN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: HAN stands for Home Area Network — used within a home connecting home devices.

mcqs.q: Home Wi-Fi connecting your devices forms which type of network?
mcqs.explanation: Home Wi-Fi connecting your devices is a Home Area Network (HAN).

mcqs.q: VPN stands for:
mcqs.explanation: VPN stands for Virtual Private Network — extends a private network across a public network.

mcqs.q: A VPN extends:
options: A public network across a private network
A private network across a public network
A LAN across a single room
A printer queue across multiple machines
mcqs.explanation: A VPN (Virtual Private Network) extends a private network across a public network (e.g. the Internet) for secure remote access.

mcqs.q: Which of the following networks covers the LARGEST area?
mcqs.explanation: WAN (Wide Area Network) covers the largest area — state, country or worldwide. The Internet is the biggest WAN.

mcqs.q: Which of the following networks covers the SMALLEST area?
mcqs.explanation: PAN (Personal Area Network) covers the smallest area — typically devices near a single person, e.g. Bluetooth.

mcqs.q: A network topology is best defined as:
options: The arrangement of nodes and connections in a network
mcqs.explanation: A network topology is the arrangement of nodes and connections in a network — it defines the physical and logical layout.

mcqs.q: In which topology do all devices connect to a single central hub or switch?
mcqs.explanation: In a Star topology, all devices connect to a single central hub or switch — the most common LAN layout.

mcqs.q: In which topology do all devices share a single backbone cable?
mcqs.explanation: In a Bus topology, all devices share a single backbone cable. If the backbone breaks, the entire network fails.

mcqs.q: In which topology does each device connect to two others, forming a closed loop?
mcqs.explanation: In a Ring topology, each device connects to exactly two other devices forming a closed loop.

mcqs.q: Which topology connects every device to every other device, providing maximum redundancy?
mcqs.explanation: A Mesh topology connects every device to every other device — providing maximum redundancy but requiring extensive cabling.

mcqs.q: Which topology is a hierarchical combination of star and bus?
mcqs.explanation: A Tree topology is a hierarchical combination of star and bus — branches of star networks connected via a backbone.

mcqs.q: A Hybrid topology is:
options: The same as a Bus topology
A combination of two or more topology types
mcqs.explanation: A Hybrid topology is a combination of two or more network topology types — common in large enterprise networks.

mcqs.q: Which topology has a single point of failure at the central hub?
mcqs.explanation: A Star topology has a single point of failure — if the central hub or switch fails, the entire network goes down.

mcqs.q: Which topology is the most common in modern Ethernet LANs?
mcqs.explanation: The Star topology is the most common in modern Ethernet LANs — devices connect to a central switch.

mcqs.q: Which topology has the highest fault tolerance?
mcqs.explanation: A Mesh topology has the highest fault tolerance — every device is connected to every other device, so multiple paths exist.

mcqs.q: A network server is best described as:
options: A central repository of data and programmes shared by users
mcqs.explanation: A network server is a central repository of data and various programmes that are shared by users in a network.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the correct function of a router?
options: Connects multiple nodes to a single LAN
Forwards packets between networks using routing information
mcqs.explanation: A router forwards packets between networks by processing the routing information included in each packet.

mcqs.q: What is the primary function of a hub?
options: Connecting multiple nodes within the same network
mcqs.explanation: A hub's primary function is to connect multiple nodes to the same network.

mcqs.q: A protocol stack is:
options: A list of protocols used by a system, one per layer
mcqs.explanation: A protocol stack is a list of protocols used by a system — one protocol per layer of the network model.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a 6-byte address used to uniquely identify a network device?
mcqs.explanation: A MAC address is a 6-byte (48-bit) unique identifier assigned to each network device.

mcqs.q: A network node is a device that:
mcqs.explanation: A network node is a device that originates, routes and terminates data within a network.

mcqs.q: In a broadcast network, the communication channel is:
options: Shared by all machines on the network
mcqs.explanation: In a broadcast network, the communication channel is shared by all machines on the network.

mcqs.q: Which type of network requires that each packet contain the full source and destination address?
mcqs.explanation: A Virtual Circuit Network requires each packet to contain the full source and destination address.

mcqs.q: Which device operates primarily at the network layer (Layer 3)?
mcqs.explanation: A router operates primarily at Layer 3 (Network Layer) — it makes forwarding decisions based on IP addresses.

mcqs.q: Which device operates at the physical layer (Layer 1)?
mcqs.explanation: A hub operates at Layer 1 (Physical Layer) — it simply repeats incoming signals to all other ports.

mcqs.q: Network nodes can include which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Network nodes include all devices that originate, route or terminate data — computers, printers, routers, servers, etc.

mcqs.q: Which of the following best describes Internet?
mcqs.explanation: The Internet is the largest example of a WAN (Wide Area Network) — it spans the whole world.

mcqs.q: A LAN card (NIC) is required when:
options: The computer needs to connect to a network
mcqs.explanation: A LAN card (Network Interface Card) is required when the computer needs to connect to a network. It is NOT necessary for a standalone computer.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is true about a router?
options: It connects nodes within a single LAN only
It forwards packets between different networks
It is the same as a hub
mcqs.explanation: A router forwards packets between different networks by reading the destination address in each packet's header.

mcqs.q: Which of these is the correct definition of a network?
options: A connection of fewer than 5 computers only
A collection of computers and devices connected via communications devices and transmission media
mcqs.explanation: A network is a collection of computers and devices connected via communications devices and transmission media — wired or wireless, large or small.
